rulings.Case Title: SALMA KHAN MD VS. HEALTH SYSTEMS DEPARTMENT, SAN MATEO COUNTY, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Second Mtn To Compel Pltff'S Supplemental Responses To Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories, Demands For Production Of Documents, And For Sanctions (Ccp 2023.030, 2030.290, 2030.300, 2031.300 & 2033.90)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 10, 2013 Line 1 - DEFENDANTs COUNTY OF SAN MATEO and CELIA MORENO, M.D.'s Second Motion To Compel Plaintiff'S Supplemental Responses To Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories, Demands For Production Of Documents, And For Sanctions (Ccp 2023.030, 2030.290, 2030.300, 2031.300 & 2033.90). Pro Tem Judge Elizabeth Riles, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion granted in part and denied in part. Plaintiff is to provide supplemental responses to Form Interrogatory Nos. 202.1, 204.3, 204.6, 208.1, 210.2, 210.3, 210.5, 210.6, 212.4, 212.5 and 212.7. Supplemental responses to all other interrogatories are denied. Plaintiff is to provide any documents not already produced that are identified in these interrogatory responses. Sanctions of $2,100.00 are awarded to Defendant. The responses and sanctions are due no later than 30 days from the date of this order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to eriles@brhrlaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ANGOTTI & REILLY, INC. VS. RINCON RESIDENTIAL TOWERS, LLC
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Amend Judgment To Add Richard D Cohen As Alter Ego
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, September 10, 2013, Line 2, PETITIONER ANGOTTI & REILLY, INC.'s Motion To Amend Judgment To Add Richard D Cohen As Alter Ego. Hearing required. Counsel are requested to be prepared to address whether Florida or California law applies, and whether the outcome is different if Florida law applies. Defendant cites Wallace ex rel Cencom Cable Income Partners II, Inc. v. Wood, 752 A.2d 1175 (Del. Ch. 1999) in support of its argument for the applicable standard under Florida law. Plaintiff does not address that case in its brief, and cites cases that predate it in arguing that Florida and California law is the same. Wallace has been cited by the Delaware Supreme Court. (See Cross v. BCBSD, Inc., 836 A.2d 492, 497 (Del. 2003) (to pierce corporate veil moving party must plead "facts supporting an inference that the corporation, through its alter-ego, has created a sham entity designed to defraud investors and creditors").) =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID W. GUERDAT VS. SALLY A. UNTERBERGER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff David Guerdat'S Motion To Compel Discovery And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 10, 2013 Line 6 - Plaintiff David Guerdat'S Motion To Compel Discovery And Request For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Elizabeth Riles, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion is granted. Defendant is ordered to appear for her deposition on September 13, 2013 or on a week day selected by Plaintiff between the date of this order and September 30, 2013. Additionally, Defendant and counsel are ordered to pay sanctions of $2,160.00. The Court notes that the Discovery Act does not recognize priority of depositions. ¿The fact that a party is conducting discovery, . . . shall not operate to delay the discovery of any other party.¿ CCP sec. 2019.020(a). See also Young v. Rosenthal (1989) 212 Cal. App. 3d 96. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to eriles@brhrlaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ALAN BYARD ET AL VS. THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A PUBLIC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant The City And County Of San Francisco, A Public, The San Francisco Police Department, A Public Motion To Strike 2nd Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, September 10, 2013, Line 10, Defendants The City And County Of San Francisco and The San Francisco Police Department's Motion To Strike 2nd Amended Complaint. Denied. Whether the 10B Program violates the City Charter § 4.127 and Police Code § 1750 may ultimately be questions of law, but the resolution may require a factual record beyond each parties' request for judicial notice. Further, much of the motion seeks to strike portions of the pleading with too fine a point at the pleading stage (e.g., page 3, line 15-16; para 11, line 5 (striking only the phrase "the Municipal Police Code"), para. 13, line 14-15 (striking only the phrase "the San Francisco Charter, Police Code")) or unnecessarily (e.g., paragraphs 9, 10). Pleadings are to be construed liberally, with a view toward substantial justice. See CCP §452. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: ALAN BYARD ET AL VS. THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A PUBLIC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant The City And County Of San Francisco, A Public, The San Francisco Police Department, A Public Demurrer To 2nd Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, September 10, 2013, Line 10, Defendants The City And County Of San Francisco and The San Francisco Police Department's Demurrer To 2nd Amended Complaint. Sustained, without leave to amend, as to Defendant CCSF and SFPD's demurrer to the first cause of action, and overruled as to the third and fourth causes of action. As to the first cause of action, a government entity is not considered a person under Bus. & Prof. § 17200, and thus cannot be sued. (People for the Ethical Treatment of Animals, Inc. v. California Milk Producers Advisory Bd. (2005) 125 Cal.App.4th 871, 879; Trinkle v. California State Lottery (1999) 71 Cal.App.4th 1198, 1202; 13 Witkin, Cal. Summary (10th ed. 2005) Equity, § 124, p. 438.) As to the third and fourth causes of action, Plaintiffs have alleged a real property interest in their "beats." at paragraph 138 of the Second Amended Complaint ("Plaintiffs have a San Francisco Charter-conferred and constitutionally protected real property right and real property in their patrol beats"), which is incorporated by reference in paragraphs 115 (third cause of action) and 128 (fourth cause of action). For purposes of demurrer, the allegations of the complaint are taken as true. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: LAZAR SHAPIRO et al VS. ALEXANDRA FRIEDMAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Alexandra Friedman'S Demurrer To 1st Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, September 10, 2013, Line 15, Defendant Alexandra Friedman's Demurrer To 1st Amended Complaint. The demurrer is sustained with leave to amend in part. The demurrer is sustained as to plaintiff Zakhrabova on the fourth cause of action for financial elder abuse for her to allege that she falls within the ambit of the statute. The Court overrules defendant's remaining arguments. The amended complaint does not clearly show that the parties entered into an illegal contract and defendant highlights specific intent crimes. The terms of the parties' contract is a factual issue. In addition, courts will sometimes enforce illegal contracts to the extent necessary to prevent unjust enrichment. See Dunkin v. Boskey (2000) 82 Cal.App.4th 171, 195-198. The Court rejects the judicial estoppel argument. The issue is whether the amended complaint has sham allegations. The allegations in the two pleadings are not totally inconsistent and the parties agreement is a factual issue. The amended complaint alleges sufficient allegations to show a fiduciary relationship and duty to plaintiff Zakhrabova. The statute of limitations does not bar this action because plaintiffs' claims accrued when defendant took money from the account. The statute of frauds does not bar plaintiff's claims because the contract could have been performed within a year. The fraud cause of action is pleaded with sufficient particularity. Plaintiff Shapiro alleges a cause of action for elder abuse because the harm occurred when he was an elder. The Court overrules the demurrers to causes of action 5 - 10 under Dunkin. In addition, plaintiffs allege conversion/harms distinct from the bank transactions at paragraph 33 of the amended complaint. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: ELIZABETH MARALES VS. WILLIAMS-SONOMA STORES, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, September 10, 2013, Line 12, PLAINTIFF ELIZABETH MARALES' DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO COMPLAINT. Off calendar. On August 27, 2013, the moving party filed a Notice of Taking the Hearing on the Demurrer to Answer Off Calendar. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: JOHNS CREEK SOFTWARE, INC. et al VS. RODENBAUGH LAW, A CALIFORNIA LAW FIRM et al*****TRANSFERRED TO SANTA CRUZ*****
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, September 10, 2013, Line 13, PLAINTIFFs JOHNS CREEK SOFTWARE, INC. and STEPHEN CHEATHAM's MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD. Granted, for good cause shown as to attorney Roger Teich. No opposition filed. To the extent the motion seeks withdrawal of attorney Howard Neu, the Court requires a separate application and declaration of counsel. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)
